standard_description: Use numbers expressed in the form of a single digit times an integer power of 10 to estimate very large or very small quantities, and to express how many times as much one is than the other. For example, estimate the population of the United States as 3 × 108 and the population of the world as 7 × 109, and determine that the world population is more than 20 times larger.
subject: Math

problems.text:
Nyan Cat travels at
$$4.2\times10^2$$
miles per hour. Grumpy Cat travels at a pokey
$$7\times10^{-1}$$
miles per hour.
a.   How many times faster is Nyan Cat traveling than Grumpy Cat?
b.   The Peregrine Falcon, the fastest bird, can fly up to half of Nyan Cat’s speed. How fast can a Peregrine Falcon fly? Write your answer in decimal notation and scientific notation.

Task
An ant has a mass of approximately $4 \times 10^{−3}$ grams and an elephant has a mass of approximately 8 metric tons.
How many ants does it take to have the same mass as an elephant?
An ant is $10^{−1}$ cm long. If you put all these ants from your answer to part (a) in a line (front to back), how long would the line be? Find two cities in the United States that are a similar distance apart to illustrate this length.
Note: 1 kg = 1000 grams, 1 metric ton = 1000 kg, 1m = 100 cm, 1km = 1000 m

A new movie is being released and it is expected to be a blockbuster. Use the information below to predict how much money the movie will make in ticket prices over the opening weekend.
The average movie ticket price in the country is $9.
There are approximately
$${4\times10^4}$$
movie screens in the country.
The movie will be playing on 30% of the screens in the country on opening weekend.
For each screen, there is an average of 250 seats, and it is expected that each showing will be sold out.
There are 8 showings per screen over the opening weekend.
Write your answer in scientific notation and standard form.

Task
A penny is about $\frac{1}{16}$ of an inch thick.
In 2011 there were approximately 5 billion pennies minted. If all of these pennies were placed in a single stack, how many miles high would that stack be?
In the past 100 years, nearly 500 billion pennies have been minted. If all of these pennies were placed in a single stack, how many miles high would that stack be?
The distance from the moon to the earth is about 239,000 miles. How many pennies would need to be in a stack in order to reach the moon?

Problem 1
The Fornax Dwarf galaxy is
$${4.6\times10^5}$$
light-years away from Earth, while Andromeda is
$${2.430\times10^6}$$
light-years away from Earth. Which is closer to Earth?
Problem 2
Subatomic particles are smaller than atoms. The average lifetime of the tau lepton particle is
$${2.906\times10^{-13}}$$
seconds, and the average lifetime of the neutral pion particle is
$${8.4\times10^{-17}}$$
seconds. Which subatomic particle has a longer average lifetime?
Problem 3
The population of Fresno, CA is approximately 500,000 people. The population of California state is approximately
$${4\times10^7}$$
. How many times greater is the population of the state than the population of the city?
Problem 4
A single piece of cat food weighs
$${4.5\times10^{-4}}$$
kg. The plastic bowl that holds the cat food weighs
$${9\times10^{-1}}$$
kg. The weight of the bowl is about how many times larger than the weight of a single piece of cat food?

Problem 1
A penny is about 0.0625 of an inch thick.
a.   In 2021 there were approximately 8 billion pennies minted. If all these pennies were placed in a single stack, how many miles high would that stack be?
b.   In the past 100 years, nearly 550 billion pennies have been minted. If all these pennies were placed in a single stack, how many miles high would that stack be?
c.   The distance from the moon to the earth is about 239,000 miles. How many pennies would need to be in a stack in order to reach the moon?
Problem 2
This headline appeared in a newspaper.
###TABLE0###
Decide whether this headline is true using the following information:
There are about
$${8\times10^3}$$
Giantburger restaurants in America.
Each restaurant serves on average
$${2.5\times10^3}$$
people every day.
There are about
$${3.2\times10^8}$$
people in America.
Explain your reasoning and show clearly how you figured it out.
Problem 3
The average mass of an adult human is about 65 kilograms, while the average mass of an ant is approximately
$${4\times10^{-3}}$$
grams. In 2010, the total human population in the world was approximately 6.84 billion, and it was estimated there were about 10,000 trillion ants alive.
Based on these values, how did the total mass of all living ants compare to the total mass of all living humans?
Problem 4
There are approximately seven billion
$${(7\times10^9)}$$
people in the world.
In the 1990’s researchers calculated that if there were just 100 people in the world:
There would be 20 children
25 people would not have food and shelter
17 people would speak Chinese
8 would speak English
a.   What fraction of people in the world did not have food and shelter? How many people in the world did not have food and shelter?
b.   How many more people in the world spoke Chinese than English?
c.   Approximately
$${3\times10^8}$$
people lived in the USA at the time of this comparison. In the world of 100 people, how many would live in the USA?
Problem 5
a.   A computer has
$$128$$
gigabytes of memory. One gigabyte is
$${1\times10^9}$$
bytes. A floppy disk, used for storage by computers in the 1970's, holds about
$$80$$
kilobytes. There are
$$1,000$$
bytes in a kilobyte. How many kilobytes of memory does a modern computer have? How many gigabytes of memory does a floppy disk have? Express your answers both as decimals and using scientific notation.
b.   George told his teacher that he spent over 21,000 seconds working on his homework. Express this amount using scientific notation. What would be a more appropriate unit of time for George to use? Explain and convert to your new units.
c.   A certain swimming pool contains about
$${3\times10^7}$$
teaspoons of water. Choose a more appropriate unit for reporting the volume of water in this swimming pool and convert from teaspoons to your chosen units.
d.   A helium atom has a diameter of about 62 picometers. There are one trillion picometers in a meter. The diameter of the sun is about 1,400,000 km. Express the diameter of a helium atom and of the sun in meters using scientific notation.  About many times larger is the diameter of the sun than the diameter of a helium atom?

Task
A penny is about $\frac{1}{16}$ of an inch thick.
In 2011 there were approximately 5 billion pennies minted. If all of these pennies were placed in a single stack, how many miles high would that stack be?
In the past 100 years, nearly 500 billion pennies have been minted. If all of these pennies were placed in a single stack, how many miles high would that stack be?
The distance from the moon to the earth is about 239,000 miles. How many pennies would need to be in a stack in order to reach the moon?

Complete the table below. Then use the letters to order the values from least to greatest.
###TABLE0###

Task
A penny is about $\frac{1}{16}$ of an inch thick.
In 2011 there were approximately 5 billion pennies minted. If all of these pennies were placed in a single stack, how many miles high would that stack be?
In the past 100 years, nearly 500 billion pennies have been minted. If all of these pennies were placed in a single stack, how many miles high would that stack be?
The distance from the moon to the earth is about 239,000 miles. How many pennies would need to be in a stack in order to reach the moon?

Problem 1
A large construction crane weighs one million pounds. A large truck weighs 10,000 pounds. How many times greater is the weight of the crane than the weight of the truck?
Problem 2
Complete the chart below. The first row has been completed for you as an example.
###TABLE0###

Task
A penny is about $\frac{1}{16}$ of an inch thick.
In 2011 there were approximately 5 billion pennies minted. If all of these pennies were placed in a single stack, how many miles high would that stack be?
In the past 100 years, nearly 500 billion pennies have been minted. If all of these pennies were placed in a single stack, how many miles high would that stack be?
The distance from the moon to the earth is about 239,000 miles. How many pennies would need to be in a stack in order to reach the moon?

Problem 1
What is the number? Write each number below in standard form.
a.
$${30,000\times10^{-1}}$$
b.
$${3\times10^3}$$
c.
$${0.3\times10^4}$$
Problem 2
Write each number described below in scientific notation.
a.   The distance from the earth to the sun is 92,955,807 miles.
b.   The mass of a grain of salt is approximately 0.0000000585 kilograms.
Problem 3
Match each number in column A to a number in column B. One number in each column is missing, which must be written in to create the remaining pairs.
###TABLE0###

Task
It is said that the average person blinks about 1000 times an hour.  This is an order-of-magnitude estimate, that is, it is an estimate given as a power of ten.  Consider:
100 blinks per hour, which is about two blinks per minute.
10,000 blinks per hour, which is about three blinks per second.
Neither of these are reasonable estimates for the number of blinks a person makes in an hour. Make order-of-magnitude estimates for each of the following:
Your age in hours.
The number of breaths you take in a year.
The number of heart beats in a lifetime.
The number of basketballs that would fill your classroom.
Can you think of others questions like these?

Task
A penny is about $\frac{1}{16}$ of an inch thick.
In 2011 there were approximately 5 billion pennies minted. If all of these pennies were placed in a single stack, how many miles high would that stack be?
In the past 100 years, nearly 500 billion pennies have been minted. If all of these pennies were placed in a single stack, how many miles high would that stack be?
The distance from the moon to the earth is about 239,000 miles. How many pennies would need to be in a stack in order to reach the moon?

Problem 1
There are about one million people living in Austin, Texas. There are about ten times as many people living in New York City, NY. Phil says that means there are one billion people living in New York City.
Explain why Phil is incorrect.
Problem 2
Read the following sentences to a peer:
Approximately 3,900,000 people live in Los Angeles, CA.
The population of the world is approximately 7,900,000,000.
A mosquito weighs about 0.0025 grams.
The average human cell weighs 0.000000000001 kg.
Scientists have estimated that there are approximately 37,200,000,000,000 cells in the human body.
Problem 3
Represent the following numbers as powers of 10 or multiples of powers of 10.
a.   One billion
b.   10,000
c.   0.001
d.   0.00000001
e.   0.00000005
f.   100,000,000
g.   500,000,000
h.   How many times bigger is one billion than 10,000?

Task
A penny is about $\frac{1}{16}$ of an inch thick.
In 2011 there were approximately 5 billion pennies minted. If all of these pennies were placed in a single stack, how many miles high would that stack be?
In the past 100 years, nearly 500 billion pennies have been minted. If all of these pennies were placed in a single stack, how many miles high would that stack be?
The distance from the moon to the earth is about 239,000 miles. How many pennies would need to be in a stack in order to reach the moon?

It is said that the average person blinks about 1000 times an hour. This is an order-of-
magnitude estimate, that is, it is an estimate given as a power of ten. Consider:
100 blinks per hour, which is about two blinks per minute.
10,000 blinks per hour, which is about three blinks per second.
Neither of these are reasonable estimates for the number of blinks a person makes in
an hour. Make order-of-magnitude estimates for each of the following:

Can you think of others questions like these?

khan_exercises.title: Negative exponents
khan_exercises.description: Practice computing numbers raised to positive and negative exponents. Bases may be fractions.
topic_path: Module 1: Integer exponents and scientific notation
Topic A: Exponential notation and properties of integer exponents

hints:
This is the definition for negative exponents:

$x^{-n}=\dfrac{1}{x^n}$

Applying the definition in the reverse direction, we get:

$\dfrac{1}{2^8}=2^{-8}$.

This is the definition for negative exponents:

$x^{-n}=\dfrac{1}{x^n}$

Applying the definition in the reverse direction, we get:

$\dfrac{1}{6^{5}}=6^{-{5}}$.

This is the definition for negative exponents:

$x^{-n}=\dfrac{1}{x^n}$

Applying the definition in the reverse direction, we get:

$\dfrac{1}{3}=\dfrac{1}{3^1}=3^{-{1}}$.

This is the definition for negative exponents:

$x^{-n}=\dfrac{1}{x^n}$

Applying the definition in the reverse direction, we get:

$\dfrac{1}{9^{2}}=9^{-{2}}$.

This is the definition for negative exponents:

$x^{-n}=\dfrac{1}{x^n}$

This is the definition for negative exponents:

$x^{-n}=\dfrac{1}{x^n}$

$x^{-n}=\dfrac{1}{x^n}$

This is the definition for negative exponents:

$x^{-n}=\dfrac{1}{x^n}$

$x^{-n}=\dfrac{1}{x^n}$

Applying the definition in the reverse direction, we get

$\dfrac{1}{a^7}=a^{-7}$.

This is the definition for negative exponents:

$x^{-n}=\dfrac{1}{x^n}$

Applying the definition in the reverse direction, we get:

$\dfrac{1}{x^{3}}=x^{-{3}}$.

This is the definition for negative exponents:

$x^{-n}=\dfrac{1}{x^n}$

Applying the definition in the reverse direction, we get:

$\dfrac{1}{y^{6}}=y^{-{6}}$.

This is the definition for negative exponents:

$x^{-n}=\dfrac{1}{x^n}$

Applying the definition in the reverse direction, we get:

$\dfrac{1}{z^{7}}=z^{-{7}}$.

This is the definition for negative exponents:

$x^{-n}=\dfrac{1}{x^n}$

This is the definition for negative exponents:

$x^{-n}=\dfrac{1}{x^n}$

This is the definition for negative exponents:

$x^{-n}=\dfrac{1}{x^n}$

This is the definition for negative exponents:

$x^{-n}=\dfrac{1}{x^n}$

khan_exercises.title: Properties of exponents challenge (integer exponents)
khan_exercises.description: A quiz that covers all exponent properties using integer exponents.
topic_path: Module 1: Integer exponents and scientific notation
Topic A: Exponential notation and properties of integer exponents

hints: $7\cdot7\cdot7\cdot7\cdot7\cdot 7$ is equivalent to $7^6$. Let's check each option and see if we can rewrite it as $7^6$ using the properties of exponents.

$\dfrac{7^{8}}{7^{2}}=7^{8-2}=7^6$

So this option is correct.

$7^6\cdot7^1=7^{6+1}=7^7$

So this option is incorrect.

$(7^2)^3=7^{2\cdot 3}=7^6$

So this option is correct.

$\dfrac{7^{12}}{7^{2}}=7^{12-2}=7^{10}$

So this option is incorrect.

In conclusion, these are the correct answers:

- $\dfrac{7^{8}}{7^{2}}$

- $(7^2)^3$

$\dfrac{1}{5}\cdot \dfrac{1}{5}\cdot \dfrac{1}{5}\cdot \dfrac{1}{5}$ is equivalent to $\left(\dfrac15\right)^4$, which is equivalent to $5^{-4}$. Let's check each option and see if we can rewrite it as $5^{-4}$ using the properties of exponents.

$(5^{-2})^{2}=5^{(-2)\cdot 2}=5^{-4}$

So this option is correct.

$(5^{-4})^{0}=5^{(-4)\cdot 0}=5^0$

So this option is incorrect.

$\dfrac{5^1}{5^4}=5^{1-4}=5^{-3}$

So this option is incorrect.

$5^2\cdot 5^{-6}=5^{2+(-6)}=5^{-4}$

So this option is correct.

In conclusion, these are the correct answers:

- $(5^{-2})^{2}$

- $5^2\cdot 5^{-6}$
$6\cdot6\cdot6\cdot6\cdot6$ is equivalent to $6^5$. Let's check each option and see if we can rewrite it as $6^5$ using the properties of exponents.

$(6^2)^3=6^{2\cdot 3}=6^6$

So this option is incorrect.

$2^5\cdot 3^5=(2\cdot 3)^5=6^5$

So this option is correct.

$\dfrac{6^6}{6^1}=6^{6-1}=6^5$

So this option is correct.

$3^2\cdot 2^3$ can't be rewritten using any of the exponent properties.

So this option is incorrect.

In conclusion, these are the correct answers:

- $2^5\cdot 3^5$

- $\dfrac{6^6}{6^1}$

$\dfrac{4^{-3}}{4^{-1}}=4^{(-3)-(-1)}=4^{-2}$

So $\dfrac{4^{-3}}{4^{-1}}$ is equivalent to $4^{-2}$. Let's check each option and see if we can rewrite it as $4^{-2}$ using the properties of exponents.

$\dfrac{4^1}{4^3}=4^{1-3}=4^{-2}$

So this option is correct.

$\dfrac{1}{4^{2}}=4^{-2}$

So this option is correct as well.

$4^3\cdot 4^1=4^{3+1}=4^4$

So this option is incorrect.

$(4^{-1})^{-3}=4^{(-1)\cdot(-3)}=4^3$

So this option is incorrect as well.

In conclusion, these are the correct answers:

- $\dfrac{4^1}{4^3}$

- $\dfrac{1}{4^{2}}$

$\dfrac{2^5}{6^5}=\left(\dfrac26\right)^5=\left(\dfrac13\right)^5$

So $\dfrac{2^5}{6^5}$ is equivalent to $\left(\dfrac13\right)^5$. Let's check each option and see if we can rewrite it using the properties of exponents as $\dfrac{2^5}{6^5}$ or $\left(\dfrac13\right)^5$.

$\dfrac{1}{3}=\left(\dfrac13\right)^1$

So this option is incorrect.

$3^{-5}=\left(\dfrac13\right)^{-1\cdot(-5)}=\left(\dfrac13\right)^5$

So this option is correct.

$(-4)^{-5}=\left(-\dfrac14\right)^{-1\cdot(-5)}=\left(-\dfrac14\right)^5$

So this option is incorrect.

$2^5\cdot 6^{-5}=2^5\cdot\left(\dfrac16\right)^{-1\cdot(-5)}=\dfrac{2^5}{6^5}$

So this option is correct.

In conclusion, these are the correct answers:

- $3^{-5}$

- $2^5\cdot 6^{-5}$

$\dfrac{5^2}{5^8}=5^{2-8}=5^{-6}$

So $\dfrac{5^2}{5^8}$ is equivalent to $5^{-6}$. Let's check each option and see if we can rewrite it as $5^{-6}$ using the properties of exponents.

$\dfrac{1}{5^6}=(5^6)^{-1}=5^{-6}$

So this option is correct.

$1^{-6}=1$

So this option is incorrect.

$(5^2)^{-3}=5^{2\cdot(-3)}=5^{-6}$

So this option is correct.

$(5^2)^{-8}=5^{2\cdot(-8)}=5^{-16}$

So this option is incorrect.

In conclusion, these are the correct answers:

- $\dfrac{1}{5^6}$

- $(5^2)^{-3}$

$4^{-2} \cdot 7^{-2}=(4\cdot 7)^{-2}=28^{-2}$

So $4^{-2} \cdot 7^{-2}$ is equivalent to $28^{-2}$. Let's check each option and see if we can rewrite it using the properties of exponents as $4^{-2} \cdot 7^{-2}$ or $28^{-2}$.

$(4 \cdot 7)^{-4}=28^{-4}$

So this option is incorrect.

$\dfrac{1}{28^{2}}=\left(\dfrac{1}{28}\right)^2=28^{-2}$

So this option is correct.

$\dfrac{7^{-2}}{4^2}=\dfrac{1}{4^2}\cdot7^{-2}=4^{-2}\cdot7^{-2}$

So this option is correct.

$(4 \cdot 7)^{4}=28^4$

So this option is incorrect.

In conclusion, these are the correct answers:

- $\dfrac{7^{-2}}{4^2}$

- $\dfrac{1}{28^{2}}$

$5^{12}\cdot 5^8=5^{12+8}=5^{20}$

So $5^{12}\cdot 5^8$ is equivalent to $5^{20}$. Let's check each option and see if we can rewrite it as $5^{20}$ using the properties of exponents.

$25^{20}=(5^2)^{20}=5^{40}$

So this option is incorrect.

$\left( 25^{5} \right)^{4}=((5^2)^5)^4=5^{40}$

So this option is incorrect as well.

$\left( 5^3 \cdot 5^2 \right)^{4}=(5^5)^4=5^{20}$

So this option is correct.

$(5^5)^4=5^{5\cdot 4}=5^{20}$

So this option is correct as well.

In conclusion, these are the correct answers:

- $\left( 5^3 \cdot 5^2 \right)^{4}$

- $(5^5)^4$

$7^{-2}\cdot 7^6=7^{-2+6}=7^4$

So $7^{-2}\cdot 7^6$ is equivalent to $7^4$. Let's check each option and see if we can rewrite it as $7^4$ using the properties of exponents.

$\dfrac{7^2}{7^{-2}}=7^{2-(-2)}=7^4$

So this option is correct.

$\dfrac{7^6}{7^{-2}}=7^{6-(-2)}=7^8$

So this option is incorrect.

$7^{-12}$ isn't equal to $7^4$.

So this option is incorrect.

$(7^2)^2=7^{2\cdot 2}=7^4$

So this option is correct.

In conclusion, these are the correct answers:

- $\dfrac{7^2}{7^{-2}}$

- $(7^2)^2$

items.question_content:
Natalie tried to evaluate the expression $\left( 4^{-3} \cdot 2^{-3} \right)^{0}$.

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}\left( 4^{-3} \cdot 2^{-3} \right)^{0}
\\\\
&=\left( 8^{-3}\right)^{0}  &\text{Step } 1
\\\\
&= 8^{0}  &\text{Step } 2
\\\\
&=0&\text{Step } 3
\end{align}$

**Did Natalie make a mistake? If so, in which step?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's go over Natalie's solution, step by step.

In Step $1$, she rewrote $ 4^{-3} \cdot 2^{-3}$ as $8^{-3}$. This is correct:

$ 4^{-3} \cdot 2^{-3}=(4\cdot 2)^{-3}=8^{-3}$

In Step $2$, Natalie rewrote $(8^{-3})^0$ as $8^0$. This is correct:

$(8^{-3})^0=8^{(-3)\cdot 0}=8^0$
In Step $3$, Natalie evaluated  $8^0$ as $0$. This is incorrect. Any number raised to $0$ equals $1$, not $0$.
In conclusion, Natalie made a mistake in Step $3$.

choices.content: Natalie did not make a mistake.
Natalie made a mistake in Step $1$.
Natalie made a mistake in Step $2$
Natalie made a mistake in Step $3$.

items.question_content:
Neil tried to rewrite the expression $\dfrac{5^{-6}} {5^{-4}}$.

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}\dfrac{5^{-6}} {5^{-4}}
\\\\
&=5^{-6-(-4)}&\text{Step } 1
\\\\
&=5^{-2}&\text{Step } 2
\\\\
&=\dfrac{1}{5^2}&\text{Step } 3
\end{align}$

**Did Neil make a mistake? If so, in which step?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's go over Neil's solution, step by step.

In Step $1$, he rewrote $\dfrac{5^{-6}} {5^{-4}}$ as $5^{-6-(-4)}$. This is correct.
In Step $2$, Neil rewrote the exponent $-6-(-4)$ as $-2$. This is correct.

In Step $3$, Neil rewrote $5^{-2}$ as $\dfrac{1}{5^2}$. This is correct:

$5^{-2}=\left(\dfrac15\right)^2=\dfrac{1}{5^2}$
In conclusion, Neil did not make a mistake.

choices.content: Neil did not make a mistake.
Neil made a mistake in Step $1$.
Neil made a mistake in Step $2$.
Neil made a mistake in Step $3$.

items.question_content:
Chris tried to rewrite the expression $\left( 4^{-2} \cdot 4^{-3} \right)^{3}$.

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}\left( 4^{-2} \cdot 4^{-3} \right)^{3}
\\\\
&=\left( 4^{-5} \right)^{3}&\text{Step } 1
\\\\
&=4^{-2}&\text{Step } 2
\\\\
&=\dfrac{1}{4^{2}}&\text{Step } 3
\end{align}$

**Did Chris make a mistake? If so, in which step?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's go over Chris's solution, step by step.

In Step $1$, he rewrote $4^{-2} \cdot 4^{-3}$ as $4^{-5}$. This is correct:

$4^{-2} \cdot 4^{-3}=4^{(-2)+(-3)}=4^{-5}$

In Step $2$, Chris rewrote $( 4^{-5})^3$ as $4^{-2}$. This is incorrect:

$( 4^{-5})^3=4^{(-5)\cdot 3}=4^{-15}$
In conclusion, Chris made a mistake in Step $2$.

choices.content: Chris did not make a mistake.
Chris made a mistake in Step $1$.
Chris made a mistake in Step $2$.
Chris  made a mistake in Step $3$.

khan_exercises.title: Powers of powers
khan_exercises.description: Rewrite powers of powers. For example, (x²)⁵ can be written as x¹⁰.

hints: The general rule for powers of powers is $\left(x^m\right)^{n}=x^{m\cdot n}$.
The general rule for powers of powers is $\left(x^m\right)^{n}=x^{m\cdot n}$.
The general rule for powers of powers is $\left(x^m\right)^{n}=x^{m\cdot n}$.
The general rule for powers of powers is $\left(x^m\right)^{n}=x^{m\cdot n}$.
The general rule for powers of powers is $\left(x^m\right)^{n}=x^{m\cdot n}$.
The general rule for powers of powers is $\left(x^m\right)^{n}=x^{m\cdot n}$.

items.question_content:
**Simplify**.  
*Rewrite the expression in the form $4^n$.*

$\left(4^2\right)^{4}=$ [[☃ expression 1]]

$\left(9^2\right)^{5}=$ [[☃ expression 1]]

$\left(y^2\right)^{3}=$ [[☃ expression 1]]

**Simplify**.  
*Rewrite the expression in the form $b^n$.*

$\left(b^3\right)^{2}=$ [[☃ expression 1]]

**Simplify**.  
*Rewrite the expression in the form $x^n$.*

$\left(x^2\right)^{4}=$ [[☃ expression 1]]

**Simplify**.  
*Rewrite the expression in the form $a^n$.*

$\left(a^5\right)^{2}=$ [[☃ expression 1]]

**Simplify**.  
*Rewrite the expression in the form $y^n$.*

$\left(y^4\right)^{2}=$ [[☃ expression 1]]

**Simplify**.  
*Rewrite the expression in the form $z^n$.*

$\left(z^1\right)^{2}=$ [[☃ expression 1]]

khan_exercises.title: Negative exponents
khan_exercises.description: Practice computing numbers raised to positive and negative exponents. Bases may be fractions.

hints:
This is the definition for negative exponents:

$x^{-n}=\dfrac{1}{x^n}$

Applying the definition in the reverse direction, we get:

$\dfrac{1}{2^8}=2^{-8}$.

This is the definition for negative exponents:

$x^{-n}=\dfrac{1}{x^n}$

Applying the definition in the reverse direction, we get:

$\dfrac{1}{6^{5}}=6^{-{5}}$.

This is the definition for negative exponents:

$x^{-n}=\dfrac{1}{x^n}$

Applying the definition in the reverse direction, we get:

$\dfrac{1}{3}=\dfrac{1}{3^1}=3^{-{1}}$.

This is the definition for negative exponents:

$x^{-n}=\dfrac{1}{x^n}$

$\dfrac{1}{9^{2}}=9^{-{2}}$.

$x^{-n}=\dfrac{1}{x^n}$

$x^{-n}=\dfrac{1}{x^n}$

Applying the definition in the reverse direction, we get

$\dfrac{1}{a^7}=a^{-7}$.

This is the definition for negative exponents:

$x^{-n}=\dfrac{1}{x^n}$

Applying the definition in the reverse direction, we get:

$\dfrac{1}{x^{3}}=x^{-{3}}$.

This is the definition for negative exponents:

$x^{-n}=\dfrac{1}{x^n}$

Applying the definition in the reverse direction, we get:

$\dfrac{1}{y^{6}}=y^{-{6}}$.

This is the definition for negative exponents:

$x^{-n}=\dfrac{1}{x^n}$

Applying the definition in the reverse direction, we get:

$\dfrac{1}{z^{7}}=z^{-{7}}$.

This is the definition for negative exponents:

$x^{-n}=\dfrac{1}{x^n}$

This is the definition for negative exponents:

$x^{-n}=\dfrac{1}{x^n}$

This is the definition for negative exponents:

$x^{-n}=\dfrac{1}{x^n}$

This is the definition for negative exponents:

$x^{-n}=\dfrac{1}{x^n}$

items.question_content:
** Express this number in scientific notation. **

 $0.5401$

[[☃ expression 1]]
hints: Count the zeroes to the right of the decimal point before the leading ${5}$: there are $0$ zeroes.
If you count the leading digit ${5}$, there is ${1}$ digit to the right of the decimal point.

khan_exercises.title: Powers of products & quotients
khan_exercises.description: Rewrite powers of products and quotients. For example, (3⁵⋅x³)² can be written as 3¹⁰⋅x⁶.

items.question_content:
**Select the three expressions that are equivalent to $4^{10}$.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
$\begin{align} \left(4^5\right)^2 &= 4^5\cdot4^5 \\
\\
 &= 4^{5+5}\\
\\
 &\stackrel\checkmark= 4^{10}\end{align}$

$\begin{align} 10\cdot 10\cdot 10\cdot 10&=10^4 \\
\\ 
 &\neq 4^{10}  \end{align}$

$\begin{align}\dfrac{4^{20}}{4^{10}}&= 4^{20-10}\\
\\
 &\stackrel\checkmark= 4^{10} 
\end{align}$

$\begin{align} 4^2 \cdot 4^5 &= 4^{2+5}\\
\\
&= 4^7 \\
\\
&\neq4^{10}
 \end{align}$

$\begin{align} (4^2\cdot 4^3)^2 &= \left(4^2\cdot4^3\right)\cdot \left(4^2\cdot4^3\right)\\
\\
 &=(4^{2+3}) \cdot (4^{2+3}) \\
\\
&= 4^5\cdot 4^5\\
\\
&=4^{5+5}\\
\\
&\stackrel\checkmark=4^{10}\end{align}$  

The following three expressions are equivalent to $4^{10}$:  

$(4^5)^2$

$\dfrac{4^{20}}{4^{10}}$

$(4^2\cdot 4^3)^2$

items.question_content:
**Select the three expressions that are equivalent to $9^{4}$.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The following three expressions are equivalent to $9^{4}$:    

$9\cdot 9\cdot 9\cdot 9$

$\left(9^2\cdot9^2\right)^1$

$\left(\dfrac{9^{10}}{9^8}\right)^2$

items.question_content:
**Select the three expressions that are equivalent to $6^{2}$.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The following three expressions are equivalent to $6^{2}$:    

$\left(\dfrac{6^9}{6^8}\right)^2$

$\dfrac{6^{4}}{6^2}$

$\dfrac{6^5\cdot6^7}{6^{10}}$

items.question_content:
**Select the two expressions that are equivalent to $z^{8}$.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The following two expressions are equivalent to $z^{8}$:    

$\left(z\cdot z^3\right)^2$

$z^6 \cdot z^2$

items.question_content:
**Select the two expressions that are equivalent to $b^{9}$.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The following expressions are equivalent to $b^9$:    

$b^6 \cdot b^3$

$\dfrac{b^{10}\cdot b^8}{b^9}$

items.question_content:

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
$\begin{align} \left(4^5\right)^2 &= 4^5\cdot4^5 \\
\\
 &= 4^{5+5}\\
\\
 &\stackrel\checkmark= 4^{10}\end{align}$

$\begin{align} 10\cdot 10\cdot 10\cdot 10&=10^4 \\
\\ 
 &\neq 4^{10}  \end{align}$

$\begin{align}\dfrac{4^{20}}{4^{10}}&= 4^{20-10}\\
\\
 &\stackrel\checkmark= 4^{10} 
\end{align}$

$\begin{align} 4^2 \cdot 4^5 &= 4^{2+5}\\
\\
&= 4^7 \\
\\
&\neq4^{10}
 \end{align}$

$\begin{align} (4^2\cdot 4^3)^2 &= \left(4^2\cdot4^3\right)\cdot \left(4^2\cdot4^3\right)\\
\\
 &=(4^{2+3}) \cdot (4^{2+3}) \\
\\
&= 4^5\cdot 4^5\\
\\
&=4^{5+5}\\
\\
&\stackrel\checkmark=4^{10}\end{align}$  

The following three expressions are equivalent to $4^{10}$:
